title: Controversies around artificial intelligence: from lawsuits to controversial statements
summary: Companies sue Microsoft and OpenAI for content use, Yann LeCun defends AI, Zuckerberg creates unwanted products, and DOJ equates Grok with clean air.

title: Analysis and launches of new electronic devices
summary: Tablets, headphones, portable consoles, 3D printers, monitors, and mechanical keyboards arrive on the market with improved specifications.

title: Highlighted tech deals: discounts on PlayStation 5, laptops, and components
summary: Amazon Prime Day, MediaMarkt, and other stores offer hardware discounts, including an RTX 5060 laptop for $1,099 and a PS5 for less than 490 euros.

title: The fintech sector moves: digital payments, cryptocurrencies, and predictions
summary: India bets on AI in payments, Binance faces issues in the EU, Polymarket and Kalshi move billions, and a fintech engineering guide is published.

title: PlayStation and Xbox sales hit historic lows as prices rise
summary: Consoles record their worst May in decades in the United States, affected by price increases and warnings that component costs will not return to normal.

title: Ford and GM replace workers with AI but face serious consequences
summary: Both automakers laid off employees to adopt automation, but lost expertise and had to rehire humans to solve the problems caused.

title: AI generates job dissatisfaction and saturation of junk content
summary: Studies and executives warn that artificial intelligence is creating a labor underclass and filling platforms with low-quality content.

title: RAM memory prices will not return to normal, manufacturers warn
summary: The increase in component costs affects everything from consoles to phones, while companies seek alternatives such as chip recycling and agreements with China.

title: The disappearance of physical discs worries gamers
summary: PlayStation eliminates digital licenses, GTA 6 will not have a disc version, and the debate over game ownership grows.

title: OpenAI and Anthropic present new versions of their AI models
summary: GPT-5.6 Sol and Claude Mythos 5 arrive with controversies over safety and cheating in tests, while Asian startups launch similar alternatives.

title: Multiple security incidents expose data and vulnerabilities
summary: From operator leaks to Russian intelligence attacks and zero-day exploits, cybersecurity faces growing challenges.

title: New studies link health to unexpected factors such as loneliness and diet
summary: Recent research reveals that social isolation is a risk for dozens of diseases, space lettuce can be dangerous, and a cannabis compound relieves pain without psychoactive effects.

title: The gaming world analyzes trends and peripherals
summary: From PS4 emulators to self-charging controllers, through debates on infinite games and VESA mounts, the industry continues to evolve.

title: Intel Nova Lake processors will consume up to 474 watts
summary: The upcoming 52-core CPUs will require three 8-pin connectors, according to leaks of Z990 motherboards.

title: Resellers speculate with Steam Machine reservations at exorbitant prices
summary: Waiting lists for Valve's console are resold on eBay for up to twice the official price, while the company studies how to make the product cheaper.

title: The high cost of video games leads to comparing subscriptions versus purchase
summary: Xbox CEO admits that gaming is unaffordable, and an economic analysis compares Game Pass, PS Plus, and individual purchase.

title: Tech regulation: Australia doubles fines, California bans loud ads
summary: Platforms face new rules on content, advertising, and moderation, while Waterloo leaves X for considering it a hostile space.

title: Digital infrastructure: record fiber optics, data centers, and legal disputes
summary: Fiber tests reach 1.2 Tb/s, Oracle sues utility regulators, and SoftBank questions Musk's orbital data centers.

title: New projects and tools launched by the community
summary: Dotient, Lyto, and a tool to convert websites into social spaces seek to attract users and developers.

title: Space exploration advances with hiring at SpaceX and plans to search for extraterrestrial life
summary: SpaceX maintains hiring despite the fall of its shares, while a swarm of spacecraft could search for life in 2040.
